answers.jumpstartProcedure: Connect cables safely and verify polarity.
answers.specialtyVehicleJack: Use the correct specialty jack or pad adapter.
answers.spoolDefinition: A jack pad or lift point adapter/spool.
answers.frozenLugNut: Use penetrating oil and controlled leverage.
answers.lockoutTools: Air wedge, long reach tool, and protective tools.
answers.lockoutDamagePrevention: Protect seals and trim and work slowly.
answers.incorrectLockoutDamage: Broken glass, bent frame, scratched paint, damaged weather seals.
answers.tirePlugKnowledge: Yes, when safe and within policy.
answers.severeDamageDecision: Inform the customer of possible damage and do not complete the unsafe service.

provider.liabilityStatement: Independent providers are responsible for civil or criminal damages resulting from their services.

confirmation.subject: AW Roadside subscription confirmed
confirmation.body:
Hello Mobile Subscriber 1778091587881,

Your AW Roadside subscription is now active.
Membership price: $7.99/month
Vehicle on file: 2020 Honda Civic Blue
Confirmation date: 2026-05-06T18:19:49.084Z

Keep this confirmation for your records.
confirmation.message: SMTP mail transport is not configured.

subscriber.platformLiability: The platform is liable for dispatch coordination only.
subscriber.providerLiability: Independent providers are responsible for civil or criminal damages resulting from their services.

answers.jumpstartProcedure: Use approved cables and follow manufacturer-safe polarity steps.
answers.jackPlacement: Use the manufacturer lift points on level ground.
answers.specialtyVehicleJack: Use the correct low-profile or specialty jack for the platform.
answers.spoolDefinition: A spool is a lift-point adapter used on some vehicles.
answers.frozenLugNut: Use penetrating oil, the right socket, and controlled leverage.
answers.lockoutTools: Air wedge, long-reach tool, and protective sleeve.
answers.lockoutDamagePrevention: Protect trim and glass and control tool pressure.
answers.incorrectLockoutDamage: It can damage seals, trim, glass, and locks.
answers.tirePlugKnowledge: I inspect the tire and plug only safe repair zones.
answers.severeDamageDecision: I inform the customer of possible damage and do not complete unsafe work.

provider.liabilityStatement: Independent providers are responsible for civil or criminal damages resulting from their services.

answers.jumpstartProcedure: Connect positive to positive, negative to ground, start donor, then disabled vehicle.
answers.jackPlacement: Use the manufacturer jack points on the pinch weld or frame support.
answers.specialtyVehicleJack: Use the vehicle-specific pad or low-profile jack that matches the lift points.
answers.spoolDefinition: A spool is a lift/jack support point adapter used on some vehicles.
answers.frozenLugNut: Use penetrating oil, correct socket fit, leverage, and controlled heat only when appropriate.
answers.lockoutTools: Air wedge, long reach tool, protective sleeve, and verified manufacturer-safe entry tools.
answers.lockoutDamagePrevention: Protect seals and paint, control tool angles, and follow the least-invasive entry method.
answers.incorrectLockoutDamage: Weather-strip damage, scratched trim, broken glass, bent frame sections, and airbag risk.
answers.tirePlugKnowledge: Yes, after checking whether the puncture is repairable and not sidewall damage.
answers.severeDamageDecision: Inform the customer of the possible damage and do not complete the service.

provider.liabilityStatement: Independent providers are responsible for civil or criminal damages resulting from their services.
